content: Unilever demonstrates strong credibility on GHG emissions, with quantified disclosure of a 77% reduction against its 2030 target and clean regulatory standing across EU ETS records. Talent signals suggest alignment, though the moderate sample size (n=12) warrants some caution in interpretation. The absence of identifiable monetary commitments in the words-versus-money stream neither supports nor undermines the company's stated climate ambitions, but represents a gap worth monitoring given the scale of decarbonization required.
